example.code:
def parse(text: str) -> Transaction: """Parse transaction from text.
:param text: Transaction text to parse :return: Parsed transaction object :raises ValueError: If text is invalid """

rule.desc: Document all public functions, classes, and modules.

examples.example: Every `def` in public API has a docstring.

rule.desc: Every module has a docstring explaining its purpose.

examples.example: `"""CSV data parser module."""` at top of file.

rule.desc: First line of docstring is a brief summary, then a blank line, then details.
Type hints document expected types; don't repeat in docstrings.

examples.example: Don't write `:param text: str - the text` when signature has `text: str`.

rule.desc: Include usage examples in docstrings for complex functions.

rule.tool_support: ruff E501 (relaxed for notebooks in pyproject.toml).

rule.desc: Two blank lines between top-level definitions, one within classes.
Use underscore prefix only for internal implementation details. Attributes that represent observable state or configuration should be public with a read-only contract.

notes.note: 1. Helper methods implementing public methods 2. Internal caches and performance optimizations 3. Internal data structures for public APIs 4. Runtime guards and one-time flags
1. Configuration parameters passed to `__init__` 2. Observable state external code needs to read 3. Computed properties already exposed via `@property`
